summary: My Son Sanctuary is a UNESCO World Heritage Site in central Vietnam, featuring a complex of ancient Hindu temples built by the Champa Kingdom between the 4th and 14th centuries. Located in a valley in Quang Nam province, the site is dedicated to the worship of Shiva and showcases unique Cham architecture with intricate carvings. It is now a historical and cultural landmark that has undergone extensive restoration, especially after damage from the Vietnam War.
itinerary: My Son Sanctuary is a UNESCO World Heritage Site in central Vietnam, featuring a complex of ancient Hindu temples built by the Champa Kingdom between the 4th and 14th centuries. name: My Son Sanctuary

summary: A peaceful and authentic cycling experience through Hue’s hidden villages, rice paddies, and local craft workshops — perfect for travelers who want to see the real Vietnam beyond the city.
name: Cycling Tour Through Hue Countryside
itinerary:
Detailed Itinerary

Morning:
Start at your hotel around 8:00 AM. After a quick bike fitting, ride along the Perfume River and cross the quiet countryside roads. Visit a local market and enjoy the morning rhythm of village life.

Midday:
Stop at Thanh Toan covered bridge and visit the agricultural museum. Taste local snacks and fresh sugarcane juice prepared by friendly villagers.

Afternoon:
Continue cycling to traditional villages where locals make conical hats and incense. Stop for a home-cooked lunch at a family house before returning to the city around 3:00 PM.

Distance: 25 – 30 km
Level: Easy to moderate
Best for: Culture lovers, outdoor enthusiasts, photographers
